title: Uvaria pisocarpa
taxonomy: Uvaria pisocarpa is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This family is part of the larger order Magnoliales, which consists of various species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Uvaria pisocarpa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. Further classification of Uvaria pisocarpa may also include its specific species and cultivars.
uses: Uvaria pisocarpa is reported to have various medicinal properties, with uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
